challenge_points.description: Analysis cites Arrighi's Long Twentieth Century but makes no link to the 'Empire or Imperialism?' debate thread or its implications for Kotkin's positional-swap claim.
Claim that dependency sources were 'not integrated' understates Martins 2022 and Treacy 2022, which already supply direct structural counter-evidence to contingent-error framing.
Mackinder thread rests on 2015-2023 derivative papers and Wikipedia; no post-2022 empirical tests of Heartland claims against current Russia-China rail and resource data.
No examination of whether Arrighi's financial-expansion phase alters the landpower advantages Mackinder attributed to Heartland control.

first_pass_summary: The research found substantial engagement with Giovanni Arrighi's systemic cycles framework but limited direct evidence on the core "positional swap" question as Kotkin frames it. Across 13 sessions, the most frequently surfaced sources were Arrighi's The Long Twentieth Century and related work on systemic cycles of accumulation. These sources address historical precedents for hegemonic transitions and industrial capacity shifts, but they operate at a different analytical level than Kotkin's spe…
